title: lobelia dunbariae subsp. paniculata
intro: Lobelia dunbariae subsp. paniculata, also known as Indian tobacco or Lobelia, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Campanulaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, due to its high concentration of alkaloids like lobeline. The health benefits of Lobelia include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce anxiety and stress, promote relaxation, and aid in weight loss. In herbal preparations, Lobelia is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be smoked or used in topical applications, although caution should be exercised due to its potential toxicity and side effects.
